missionStatement: At Metropolitan Community College, we are committed to equipping students with the critical thinking skills, resourcefulness and knowledge that add value to themselves, their families and the community. We bring together the latest technologies, diverse cultural backgrounds and dynamic learning environment to identify solutions to the complex global problems of the 21st Century.
advantages: In providing the path to educational success, the college offers excellent educational opportunities in vocational and academic studies.
In eliminating barriers to career success, course delivery is geared towards benefiting learners of all social and cultural backgrounds.

In promoting collegiality in educational and career success, courses are taught in a collegial atmosphere where consistent standards of integrity and educational competence are maintained at all times.
Both our mission statement and educational objectives are driven by our core values — based on the belief that every person must have effective access to lifelong learning.

home.title: Metropolitan Community College | English ESL | MCC | Esports Program | Vancouver
home.hero: We are pleased to welcome students, visitors and faculty members to Metropolitan Community College. Metropolitan Community College is fully registered and designated by the Private Training Institutions Branch (PTIB) of the Ministry of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills in British Columbia.
home.latestNews: Metropolitan Community College welcomes all visitors to our campus. Our website is currently under development for updates of new upcoming programs. This will not affect regular usage of the website. For appointments, please leave a message on our website or inform us by email at info@metropolitancollege.ca or by filling in the Contact Form. For program-related information, please refer to our Programs page. For all applications, please refer to our Admissions page.

featuredPrograms.name: CELPIP Test Preparation
featuredPrograms.blurb: Our CELPIP Test Preparation course is a 3-week intense course. We guarantee at least 1 CLB level improvement for all of our students. *some conditions may apply.

featuredPrograms.name: Hospitality Diploma Program
featuredPrograms.blurb: Our Hospitality program can be taken full-time or part-time in one or two years. Graduates have a variety of career options after completion. Pathway to Degree Programs. Potential Triple Credentials – AHLA, MCC and UCW.

featuredPrograms.name: Esports Diploma Program
featuredPrograms.blurb: The one and only esports management program in Canada, the International Esports Management Diploma program is targeted to provide students with management knowledge focused on all aspects of the ever-growing e-sport industry.

about.title: About MCC
about.story: Established in 1993, Metropolitan Community College is a Canadian post-secondary institution registered with and designated by the Private Training Institutions Regulatory Unit (PTIRU) of the Ministry of Post-Secondary Education and Future Skills in British Columbia. The College holds Designated Learning Institution (DLI #O19329963492) status from Immigration, Refugees and Citizenship Canada. International students who want to study in Canada may apply for study permits. Students in most programs can work part-time up to 20 hours weekly while studying full-time. The institution is recognized by British Columbia Education Quality Assurance (BC EQA). Programs feature small class sizes with interactive teaching. The Advisory Board regularly examines program competencies to ensure student success.
about.campus: Located at 100 - 322 Water Street, Vancouver, BC, the main campus occupies over 4,200 square feet including four classrooms, one seminar room, one computer lab, two offices, and a student lounge with kitchen. Both in-person and distance education are offered. Campus tours are available for prospective students.
about.shortAbout: Since its foundation in 1993, Metropolitan Community College has established itself as a community of learners, working in partnership with other institutes of higher education, agencies and organizations in providing affordable educational opportunities to students of all social backgrounds and different geographic locations. Courses are taught in a collegial atmosphere that fosters small class sizes for efficient instructor-student interaction.

missionStatement.title: Mission Statement
missionStatement.body: At Metropolitan Community College, we are committed to equipping students with the critical thinking skills, resourcefulness and knowledge that add value to themselves, their families and the community. We bring together the latest technologies, diverse cultural backgrounds and dynamic learning environment to identify solutions to the complex global problems of the 21st Century.

pillars: Excellent educational opportunities in vocational and academic studies
Elimination of barriers to career success
Course delivery benefiting learners of all social and cultural backgrounds
Consistent standards of integrity and educational competence

programsOverview.title: Programs
programsOverview.intro: Programs Overview — explore MCC's vocational and academic programs.

fourStepFlow.text: Look at our list of programs to know in which field you want to study and to choose the ones that fit best.
Visit our admission procedure page to know the admission process.
Start out by filling the application form so we can understand your needs better to help you become a part of MCC.
Join our Facebook page and other social networking sites to stay updated.

admissionProcedure.title: Admission Procedure
admissionProcedure.intro: We encourage prospective students to review our program offerings and welcome qualified students to apply for admissions to our programs.

steps.text: Submit the online application form and pay the application fee.
Submit required documents including passport/ID, transcripts, proof of English proficiency, and study permit or visitor visa if applicable.
We will review the application and issue an Offer of Admissions if admission requirements are met.
Upon receiving the payment of tuition fees, we will issue an official Letter of Acceptance (LOA) stating the amount received and details of program enrollment.

admissionProcedure.note: International students can use the official LOA to apply for a Study Permit before the commencement of the program.

admissionRequirements.title: Admission Requirements
admissionRequirements.general: In general, applicants should be over 18 years old, have completed high school education, and have attained an acceptable level of proficiency in the English language. International applicants must obtain a study permit for any program longer than 6 months.

perProgram.advancedParalegal: Completion of high school or mature student status (over 18). Evidence of IELTS 5.5 or equivalent (TOEFL IBT). Alternatively, complete the Business Communication or EPAS program, or pass the College's language proficiency exam with at least 70%.
perProgram.businessCommunications: Generally, an IELTS 5.0 or equivalent (may take a college diagnostic test). Students bridging into diploma studies must satisfy the target diploma program requirements.
perProgram.celpipPreparation: Previous CELPIP Test Score, OR take a CELPIP Diagnostic Test at the college.
perProgram.hospitalityOperations: At least 18 years old. Evidence of IELTS 5.5 or equivalent (TOEFL IBT), or pass the College's language proficiency exam with at least 70%.
perProgram.globalManagementInHospitality: At least 18 years old. Evidence of IELTS 5.5 or equivalent (TOEFL IBT), or pass the College's language proficiency exam with at least 70%.
perProgram.internationalEsportsManagement: Completion of high school or mature student status (over 18). IELTS-A 5.5 with no band score lower than 5.0 (or equivalent TOEFL/CAEL). Alternative: Language Proficiency Test or completion of Business Communication program.

tuitionFeesPayments.title: Tuition, Fees & Payments
tuitionFeesPayments.note: Refer to the Programs Page for updated tuition and program-related fees in each program.

academicCalendar.title: Academic Calendar
academicCalendar.intro: MCC Vancouver Campus Academic Calendar serves as official documentation regarding programs and courses. Beginning January 1, 2022, MCC implemented standard scheduling across most diploma programs with semesters: Fall (September - December), Winter (January - April), and Summer (May - August). Some programs and part-time offerings may operate on different schedules. Attendance is mandatory from the first through final day of each semester for successful course completion.
holidays: National Day for Truth and Reconciliation

arrivalInCanada.title: Arrival in Canada
headings: If you have applied for a Study Permit and got an Approval Letter
If you are a short-term visiting student or already have a Study Permit
General Information Before Travelling to Canada
Key Documents to Travel to Canada
If you arrive in Canada by AIR
If you arrive in Canada by LAND
Virtual Campus Tours and Online Webinars
Campus Tours and Social Networking Events
Introduction to Canadian Culture and Laws
keyDocuments: A valid Electronic Travel Authorization (eTA) or Temporary Resident Visa (TRV)
A valid Study Permit or a port of entry (POE) letter
A Confirmation of Enrolment (COE) if you are a current student
If required, valid immigration medical exam results
whatToBring: Identity document from your country (e.g. driver's license)
Marriage certificate and English translation (if applicable)
Credit card and/or cash (declare if over CAD 10,000)
arrivalInCanada.portOfEntryNote: The CBSA officer will ask to see travel documents, ask questions, and confirm eligibility. Entry remains at the discretion of a CBSA officer.

housingAndAccommodations.title: Housing & Accommodations
housingAndAccommodations.intro: Although on-campus housing is not available, there are various housing options for students to consider. MCC offers free assistance through admin@metropolitancollege.ca.

options.description: Affordable housing and meal package with the opportunity to learn Canadian culture through screened host families. Pricing varies.
Modern, fully furnished accommodations operated by third-party providers (e.g. GEC).
Direct rental options. The college usually does not encourage new students to rent an apartment directly.

healthAndSafety.title: Health & Safety
headings: Why MSP Is Essential for International Students

insurance.requirement: All international students in British Columbia are required to have valid medical insurance coverage. Students must obtain private coverage within their first 90 days before becoming eligible for provincial plans.

msp.description: BC Medical Service Plan — the province's health insurance program providing essential medical coverage for residents, including international students staying over six months.
msp.process: Apply online via the BC government website. Three-month processing period. Receive BC Services Card upon approval.

mentalHealth: Here2Talk program (Government of BC) — confidential counseling
Regular workshops on stress management and work-life balance
healthAndSafety.addictionsSupport: Naloxone kits are available at our campus to address opioid-related emergencies. Students can request kits discreetly at the front desk with instructions provided. The Student Support Team offers assistance and referrals to recovery centers.

emergency.publicEmergency: Dial 911 in Canada for police, fire, and ambulance.
emergency.campusSecurity: Security services provided by third-party contractors with passcode-protected entrance. The Student Support team is available to assist with safety concerns or emergencies.

financialAid.title: Financial Aid, Scholarships & Bursaries
financialAid.intro: We understand the situation and are here to help you achieve your academic goal by providing financial assistance opportunities such as scholarships and bursaries. Up to one (1) scholarship or bursary per eligible student is allowed.
financialAid.bcStudentLoan: Available through StudentAidBC — low-interest rates, flexible repayment plans, and tailored options for eligible students. Applications are processed first-come, first-served.

scholarships.name: English Excellence Scholarship for International Student
scholarships.englishRequirement: IELTS Academic overall 6.5 or other equivalent language proficiency tests

scholarships.name: Special Bursary for Turkish International Student
scholarships.applicants: New international students with Turkish passport

learningSupport: Support for students with academic difficulties

services.name: Networking

services.name: One-on-One Career Advising

livingInVancouver.title: Living in Vancouver
livingInVancouver.intro: Vancouver is one of the most spectacular cities in the world, as well as one of its cleanest and safest.
livingInVancouver.climate: Vancouver has the mildest climate of any major city in Canada. Temperatures range from 6°C to 18°C (January–May) and 17°C to 24°C (May–August).
livingInVancouver.neighbourhoodNote: MCC is situated near the Steam Clock in Gastown — one of Vancouver's oldest and most culturally diverse neighbourhoods, bordered by Chinatown and steps from public transit and Canada Place.
livingInVancouver.cellularServices: Phone plans typically require two forms of ID and proof of address.

integrationWithCommunity.title: Integration with Local Community
integrationWithCommunity.intro: Student success goes beyond academics. Our student life programs are thoughtfully designed to help students actively engage with the local Vancouver community.

pillars.name: Community-Based Clubs & Activities
pillars.description: Student-led clubs enable peers to collaborate on Vancouver-based projects, participate in language exchange, and engage with professional networking and business clubs tied to local industries.

pillars.name: Cultural Celebrations with Local Engagement
pillars.description: Inclusive cultural events including public showcases and international food fairs, in collaboration with local artists, cultural organizations, and guest speakers.

pillars.name: Volunteering & Community Leadership
pillars.description: Local volunteering with non-profits, shelters, food banks, and community centres. Civic engagement activities and leadership training programs for peer mentoring and ambassador roles.

pillars.name: Field Trips & Cultural Immersion
pillars.description: Guided tours of historical sites, art galleries, Indigenous museums, and civic institutions; visits to local businesses, government facilities, and non-profit organizations; outdoor excursions throughout Vancouver.

agentCooperation.title: Agent Cooperation
agentCooperation.intro: MCC works closely with agents worldwide to recruit qualified students. We provide promotional materials, training sessions, campus tours and other resources to support our agents in their recruitment effort. We also keep our agents posted regarding our important updates for advising prospective students.
benefitsToAgents: Ongoing updates for advising prospective students
agentCooperation.callToAction: If you are interested in cooperating with us, please fill in the Appointed Agent Application form.
agentCooperation.confirmation: Thanks for your interest in cooperating with us! We will get back to you soon.

contact.title: Contact
headings: Get in touch with us today!
contact.intro: For more information on our programs of study, student services, career opportunities or other inquiries, please contact us or complete and submit the contact form.
contact.successMessage: Thanks for contacting us! We will get back to you soon.

programs.name: Global Management in Hospitality
programs.tagline: Join a growing industry & gain opportunities worldwide
programs.description: The Global Management in Hospitality Diploma program is offered in collaboration with the American Hotel & Lodging Education Institute (AHLEI) to prepare students for various management roles in the hospitality industry. Students gain industry-specific skills in computer applications, financial management, room division management, food and beverage management, marketing and sales.
highlights: Accredited by American Hotel and Lodging Association (AHLA)
Double diplomas — from both AHLEI and MCC
Potential Triple Credentials — AHLA, MCC and UCW
Pathway to Degree Programs in as little as 3 years
admissionRequirements: Completion of high school OR mature student (18+)
Evidence of IELTS 5.5 or equivalent (e.g. TOEFL IBT)
OR successful completion of Business Communication / EPAS preparatory programs
OR pass the College's language proficiency exam with at least 70%
programs.applicationDeadline: 4 weeks prior to start of classes; late admission possible based on intake

programs.name: Hospitality Operations
programs.tagline: Join a growing industry & gain opportunities worldwide
programs.description: The Hospitality Operations Diploma program is offered in collaboration with the American Hotel & Lodging Education Institute (AHLEI) to prepare students for various roles in the hospitality industry. Students learn industry-specific skills in computer applications, finance, operations, marketing and sales. Graduates receive both a Hospitality Operations Diploma from MCC and the AHLA equivalent.
highlights: Accredited by American Hotel and Lodging Association (AHLA)
Double diplomas — from both AHLEI and MCC
Potential Triple Credentials — AHLA, MCC and UCW
Opportunity to advance to Global Hospitality Management Diploma
admissionRequirements: High school completion OR 18+ as mature student
OR successful completion of Business Communication / EPAS
OR pass college language proficiency exam with 70%
programs.applicationDeadline: 4 weeks prior to start of classes; late admission possible based on intake

programs.name: Business Communication
programs.tagline: Introduction to International Business Communication & Workplace Diversity
programs.description: The Business Communication program enhances students' communication skills in a business environment. The program consists of two courses to upgrade business writing, presentation and public speaking skills essential to succeed in today's complex business environment. Designed for non-native English speakers.
admissionRequirements: Generally an IELTS 4.0 or equivalent (may take a college diagnostic test for placement)

programs.name: CELPIP Preparation
programs.description: Our CELPIP Preparation Program offers in-depth strategies and skill-building for each component of the CELPIP-General Test. Developed in collaboration with the program administrator and delivered by experienced instructors, the program aims to improve student scores by 1–2 CLB levels.
admissionRequirements: Take a CELPIP Diagnostic Test at the College
programs.intakeStatus: Currently not intaking new students due to restructuring

programs.name: International Esports Management
programs.tagline: Be a part of the Esports Ecosystem with Metropolitan Community College
programs.uniqueClaim: The one and only academic program in Esports Management in Canada.
programs.description: The International Esports Management diploma program provides students with the opportunity to turn their passion for playing video games into a viable career in the esports industry. Students learn the history, development, concept, culture, and management of esports teams and related organizations.
admissionRequirements: Completion of high school OR 19+ as mature student
Grade C+ in English 12, OR IELTS-Academic 5.5 (no band lower than 5.0), OR equivalent language proficiency
Alternative: Language Proficiency Test or completion of Business Communication program

programs.name: Advanced Paralegal (Without Co-op)
programs.status: Discontinued — for record and reference only. No longer taking students in 2025.
programs.tagline: Dive into the world of laws…
programs.description: The Advanced Paralegal Diploma is a 50-week intensive program providing training in traditional legal administrative skills and legal procedures. Students handle and format legal correspondence such as deeds, wills, affidavits and briefs, along with civil and family litigation, under qualified instructors.
highlights: Advocacy — examination, cross-examination, submission of arguments
Ability to analyze and apply legislation to real-life situations
admissionRequirements: Completion of high school OR 18+ as mature student
OR successful completion of Business Communication / EPAS
OR pass College language proficiency exam with 70%
programs.applicationDeadline: 4 weeks prior to start of classes (program now discontinued)

missingFromSource: Testimonials — none found on any visited page
FAQ section — no dedicated FAQ page found
